# Next Tasks Queue - Psycho-Forge Development

**Project:** Psycho-Forge - The Scientific Operating System for Behavioral Research  
**Current Phase:** Sprint 2 Development (100% Complete - Ready for External Audits)  
**Last Updated:** July 10, 2025

---

## Sprint 2 COMPLETED ✅

### Sprint 2 Achievements:
- ✅ Complete Stroop Task Implementation (TST-002)
- ✅ Lock v0.1 BIDS-compatible Data Schema (ARE-003)
- ✅ Build TypeScript Fluent API & Compiler (DSL-003)
- ✅ Implement Core Stimulus Types (KER-003) with PixiJS
- ✅ Address Security Improvements from Sprint 1 Audits (SEC-001)
- ✅ External AI Audits PASSED (O3: 7/10, Gemini Pro: 9/10)

**Sprint 2 Status**: ALL DELIVERABLES COMPLETE. External audits passed. Ready for Sprint 3 advancement.

## Sprint 3: GUI View Layer - Ready to Begin

### External Audit Results Summary:
- **O3 Technical Audit**: PASSED (7/10) - High implementation quality, ready for Sprint 3
- **Gemini Pro Architecture Audit**: PASSED (9/10) - Exceptional technical execution
- **Combined Verdict**: APPROVED for Sprint 3 advancement with risk mitigation priorities

### Critical Action Items from Audits (Must Address in Sprint 3):

### 🏗️ 1. Implement Hardware Timing Validation Suite (TIMING-001)
**Status**: Ready to Start
**Assigned to**: TBD
**Due**: Sprint 3 Week 1
**Priority**: Critical
**Story Points**: 3

**Description:** Create automated hardware benchmark suite to validate <2ms timing precision claims across devices.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Implement FPS, frame variance, and heap monitoring
- [ ] Create device compatibility matrix testing
- [ ] Add requestVideoFrameCallback validation
- [ ] Test across 60Hz, 120Hz, 144Hz displays
- [ ] Document browser/OS timing limitations

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Automated timing validation runs in CI
- [ ] Clear documentation of supported configurations
- [ ] Performance regression detection
- [ ] Hardware requirement specifications

**Dependencies**: None - can run in parallel with GUI development
**Risk Level**: High - timing claims are fundamental to value proposition
**Notes**: O3 audit identified this as critical requirement before production claims

### 🏗️ 2. Add WebGL2 Feature Detection and Fallback (COMPAT-001)
**Status**: Ready to Start
**Assigned to**: TBD
**Due**: Sprint 3 Week 1
**Priority**: High
**Story Points**: 2

**Description:** Implement graceful degradation for browsers without WebGL2 support.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] WebGL2 capability detection on startup
- [ ] Canvas2D fallback renderer for basic stimuli
- [ ] Clear user messaging about performance limitations
- [ ] Support matrix documentation

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Experiments run on WebGL1/Canvas2D with reduced precision
- [ ] Users informed of performance impact
- [ ] No crashes on unsupported hardware
- [ ] Fallback tested on older browsers

**Dependencies**: None
**Risk Level**: Medium - expands device compatibility
**Notes**: Addresses hardware limitations identified in audits

### 🏗️ 3. Implement API Complexity Management (API-001)
**Status**: Ready to Start
**Assigned to**: TBD
**Due**: Sprint 3 Week 2
**Priority**: High
**Story Points**: 3

**Description:** Establish processes to prevent ForgeScript API from becoming unwieldy.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Create RFC process for new API additions
- [ ] Implement API surface linting rules
- [ ] Add JSON declarative experiment format
- [ ] Document deprecation policy
- [ ] Create migration guides

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] RFC template and process documented
- [ ] Public API changes require review
- [ ] Simple experiments can use JSON format
- [ ] Semantic versioning implemented

**Dependencies**: None
**Risk Level**: Medium - prevents future technical debt
**Notes**: O3 audit warned of "builder hell" risk at 1.3k LOC

### 🏗️ 4. Security Hardening Phase 2 (SEC-002)
**Status**: Ready to Start
**Assigned to**: TBD
**Due**: Sprint 3 Week 2
**Priority**: High
**Story Points**: 3

**Description:** Expand security implementation beyond current XSS/CSS protections.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers
- [ ] Add third-party dependency auditing
- [ ] Implement consent data encryption
- [ ] Add rate limiting and payload limits
- [ ] Security scanning in CI pipeline

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] CSP headers prevent injection attacks
- [ ] npm audit runs on every build
- [ ] Sensitive data encrypted at rest
- [ ] Security report generated per release

**Dependencies**: None
**Risk Level**: High - critical for IRB compliance
**Notes**: Gemini Pro identified gaps in current 333 LOC security layer

### 🏗️ 5. Memory Profiling and Optimization (PERF-001)
**Status**: Ready to Start
**Assigned to**: TBD
**Due**: Sprint 3 Week 3
**Priority**: Medium
**Story Points**: 2

**Description:** Add performance monitoring to prevent memory issues at scale.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Implement heap snapshot profiling
- [ ] Add toggles for verification overhead
- [ ] Create memory usage dashboards
- [ ] Implement streaming for large experiments
- [ ] Add garbage collection hints

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Memory profiling available in dev mode
- [ ] Verification can be disabled for performance
- [ ] Large experiments don't exhaust heap
- [ ] Performance metrics in telemetry

**Dependencies**: None
**Risk Level**: Medium - prevents scaling issues
**Notes**: O3 warned of 2-3x RAM spikes from verification

---

## Sprint 3 Primary Deliverables (After Risk Mitigation)

### 🏗️ 6. SvelteKit GUI Application Setup (GUI-001)
**Status**: Ready to Start
**Assigned to**: TBD
**Due**: Sprint 3 Week 1
**Priority**: Critical
**Story Points**: 5

**Description:** Initialize SvelteKit application with proper architecture and tooling.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] SvelteKit 5 with Vite configuration
- [ ] TailwindCSS + DaisyUI component library
- [ ] Dark/light theme support
- [ ] Responsive design system
- [ ] Accessibility framework (ARIA)
- [ ] State management (Svelte stores)
- [ ] Router configuration

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Development environment runs locally
- [ ] Component library integrated
- [ ] Theme switching works
- [ ] Mobile responsive layouts
- [ ] WCAG 2.2 AA compliant structure

**Dependencies**: None - foundational GUI work
**Risk Level**: Low - standard web development
**Notes**: Foundation for all Sprint 3 UI work

### 🏗️ 7. Visual Timeline Builder Component (GUI-002)
**Status**: Ready to Start
**Assigned to**: TBD
**Due**: Sprint 3 Week 2
**Priority**: Critical
**Story Points**: 8

**Description:** Create drag-and-drop timeline interface for experiment construction.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Drag-and-drop timeline with snap-to-grid
- [ ] Zoom and pan controls
- [ ] Trial blocks and stimulus items
- [ ] Property panels for editing
- [ ] Undo/redo functionality
- [ ] Timeline validation indicators
- [ ] Export to ForgeScript

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Users can visually construct experiments
- [ ] Timeline exports valid ForgeScript code
- [ ] Validation shows timing conflicts
- [ ] Smooth interactions at 60fps
- [ ] Keyboard navigation support

**Dependencies**: GUI-001 (SvelteKit setup)
**Risk Level**: High - complex interaction design
**Notes**: Core differentiator for researcher productivity

### 🏗️ 8. Monaco Editor Integration (GUI-003)
**Status**: Ready to Start
**Assigned to**: TBD
**Due**: Sprint 3 Week 3

- Web API deprecation or restriction announcements

input.content:
# @psyforge/validation

Hardware Timing Validation Suite for Psycho-Forge

## Overview

The validation package provides automated tools to verify the <2ms timing precision claims of the Psycho-Forge platform across different hardware configurations, browsers, and operating systems.

## Features

- **Hardware Benchmarking**: Comprehensive performance metrics including FPS, timing precision, and memory usage
- **Display Detection**: Automatic detection of refresh rates (60Hz, 120Hz, 144Hz, 240Hz)
- **Browser Feature Detection**: WebGL2, requestVideoFrameCallback, Web Audio API support
- **CI Integration**: Automated validation in continuous integration pipelines
- **Performance Regression Detection**: Compare against baseline metrics
- **Multiple Output Formats**: JSON, Markdown, JUnit XML

## Installation

```bash
npm install @psyforge/validation
```

## Usage

### Command Line Interface

```bash
# Run full validation
npx psyforge-validate

# Run CI validation with baseline comparison
npx psyforge-validate --mode ci --baseline baseline.json

# Save results as new baseline
npx psyforge-validate --save-baseline baseline.json

# Output JSON for processing
npx psyforge-validate --output json > results.json
```

### Programmatic API

```typescript
import { HardwareBenchmark, CIValidator } from '@psyforge/validation';

// Run hardware benchmark
const benchmark = new HardwareBenchmark();
const result = await benchmark.runFullBenchmark();

if (result.validation.passed) {
  console.log('✅ Timing precision validated!');
} else {
  console.log('❌ Timing requirements not met');
  console.log('Warnings:', result.validation.warnings);
}

// Generate compatibility matrix
const matrix = await benchmark.generateCompatibilityMatrix();
console.log('60Hz performance:', matrix.get('60Hz'));
```

### CI Integration

```yaml
# GitHub Actions example
- name: Validate Timing Precision
  run: npx psyforge-validate --mode ci --baseline baseline.json
  
- name: Upload Results
  uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
  with:
    name: timing-validation
    path: validation-report.md
```

## Validation Criteria

The validation suite checks the following criteria:

### Timing Precision (Critical)
- **Stimulus Onset Jitter**: Must be ≤2ms
- **Clock Precision**: Sub-millisecond accuracy required
- **Frame Timing Accuracy**: >95% of frames within threshold

### Performance (Required)
- **Frame Rate**: 95th percentile must be ≥58 FPS (for 60Hz displays)
- **Frame Variance**: Standard deviation ≤2ms
- **Memory Usage**: Heap usage <100MB for standard experiments

### Browser Compatibility
- **WebGL2 Support**: Required for high-performance rendering
- **RequestVideoFrameCallback**: Preferred for precise frame timing
- **Web Audio API**: Required for audio stimulus timing

## Output Formats

### Markdown Report
```markdown
# Hardware Timing Validation Report

## System Information
- Platform: Darwin
- CPU Cores: 8
- Display: 2560x1440 @ 120Hz

## Performance Results
- Stimulus Onset Jitter: 1.2ms ✅
- Frame Rate (P95): 118.5 FPS ✅
- Frame Variance: 0.8ms ✅
```

### JSON Output
```json
{
  "timestamp": 1720656000000,
  "validation": {
    "passed": true,
    "timingPrecisionMet": true
  },
  "performance": {
    "timing": {
      "stimulusOnsetJitter": 1.2,
      "clockPrecision": 0.5
    }
  }
}
```

### JUnit XML (for CI systems)
```xml
<testsuites name="PsyForge Timing Validation">
  <testsuite name="Hardware Timing">
    <testcase name="TimingPrecision" time="1.2"/>
    <testcase name="FrameRate" time="0"/>
  </testsuite>
</testsuites>
```

## Performance Recommendations

Based on validation results, the system provides specific recommendations:

- **High Jitter**: "Consider using a higher refresh rate display"
- **Frame Drops**: "Close unnecessary applications and browser tabs"
- **Memory Issues**: "Implement object pooling for stimuli"
- **GC Pauses**: "Optimize memory allocation patterns"

## Browser Support

| Browser | WebGL2 | VideoFrameCallback | Timing Precision |
|---------|--------|-------------------|------------------|
| Chrome 83+ | ✅ | ✅ | <1ms |
| Firefox 78+ | ✅ | ❌ | <2ms |
| Safari 15+ | ✅ | ❌ | <2ms |
| Edge 83+ | ✅ | ✅ | <1ms |

## Hardware Requirements

Minimum hardware for reliable <2ms timing:
- **CPU**: 4+ cores, 2.5GHz+
- **RAM**: 8GB+
- **GPU**: WebGL2 compatible
- **Display**: 60Hz minimum (120Hz+ recommended)

## Contributing

See the main repository CONTRIBUTING.md for development guidelines.

## License

MIT - Part of the Psycho-Forge project

  - **HardwareTimingValidator.ts**: Comprehensive hardware capability testing
  - **Performance Metrics**: FPS stability, frame variance, dropped frames detection
  - **Timing Precision**: Clock resolution, drift rate, jitter measurement
  - **Memory Monitoring**: Heap usage tracking during validation
  - **Compatibility Matrix**: Automated 2ms/5ms/16ms precision capability detection
  - **CI/CD Integration**: Playwright-based cross-platform automated testing
  - **Browser UI**: Interactive validation runner with real-time results
  - **Report Generation**: Human-readable reports with recommendations
- **Technical Achievements (WebGL Fallback)**:
  - **EnhancedStimulusRenderer**: Already implemented with full WebGL2/WebGL1/Canvas2D detection
  - **Automatic Fallback**: Graceful degradation when WebGL unavailable
  - **Canvas2D Renderer**: Basic shapes, text, and image rendering support
  - **Performance Warnings**: Clear messaging about reduced capabilities
  - **CapabilityChecker**: System capability detection and warning system
  - **Test Coverage**: Comprehensive test suite for fallback scenarios
  - **WebGLFallback.test.ts**: Validates all fallback functionality
- **Implementation Details**:
  - Hardware validation runs 3-5 second tests measuring actual performance
  - Supports CI integration with GitHub Actions workflow updates
  - Provides clear pass/fail criteria for timing precision requirements
  - Canvas2D fallback maintains experiment functionality on limited hardware
  - Warning system helps researchers understand limitations
  - Both features directly address critical audit feedback from Sprint 2
- **New Files Created**:
  - `packages/timing/src/HardwareTimingValidator.ts`

input.content:
# RFC-001: ForgeScript API Complexity Management

**Status**: Proposed  
**Created**: 2025-07-10  
**Author**: Psycho-Forge Development Team

## Summary

This RFC establishes processes and guidelines to prevent the ForgeScript API from becoming unwieldy as it grows, addressing the "builder hell" risk identified in the O3 audit at 1.3k LOC.

## Motivation

The O3 audit warned that fluent APIs can become complex as they grow. With ForgeScript already at 1.3k LOC, we need proactive measures to:
- Maintain API simplicity and usability
- Provide escape hatches for simple use cases
- Ensure backward compatibility
- Prevent feature creep

## Design Principles

### 1. Progressive Disclosure
- **Core API**: Essential features for 80% of use cases
- **Extended API**: Advanced features in separate modules
- **Expert API**: Low-level access for special cases

### 2. Multiple Paradigms
- **Fluent Builder**: For complex experiments
- **JSON Declarative**: For simple experiments
- **Function Composition**: For reusable components

### 3. Semantic Versioning
- **Major**: Breaking changes (avoided when possible)
- **Minor**: New features (backward compatible)
- **Patch**: Bug fixes and performance improvements

## Implementation

### API Surface Linting

Create automated checks to monitor API growth:

```typescript
// .forgescript-api-lint.json
{
  "maxPublicMethods": 100,
  "maxMethodParameters": 4,
  "maxChainDepth": 6,
  "requireDocumentation": true,
  "deprecationPolicy": {
    "minVersionsBeforeRemoval": 3,
    "requireMigrationGuide": true
  }
}
```

### JSON Declarative Format

Simple experiments should be expressible in JSON:

```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "experiment": {
    "name": "Simple Reaction Time",
    "trials": [
      {
        "stimulus": {
          "type": "text",
          "content": "+",
          "duration": 500
        },
        "response": {
          "type": "keyboard",
          "validKeys": ["space"],
          "timeout": 2000
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### API Categories

#### Core API (Always Available)
```typescript
// Essential experiment building
experiment()
  .trial()
  .stimulus()
  .response()
  .run()
```

#### Extended API (Import Separately)
```typescript
import { advanced } from '@psyforge/forgescript/advanced';

// Complex timing control
advanced.timeline()
  .parallel()
  .withJitter()
  .adaptiveTiming()
```

#### Expert API (With Warnings)
```typescript
import { unsafe } from '@psyforge/forgescript/unsafe';

// Direct hardware access
unsafe.directTimingControl()
  .bypassValidation()
  .rawHardwareAccess()
```

## RFC Process

### 1. Proposal Template

All API additions must include:

```markdown
## API Addition Proposal

### Summary
Brief description of the new API

### Motivation
Why is this needed? What problem does it solve?

### API Design
```typescript
// Proposed API signature
```

### Usage Example
```typescript
// How developers will use it
```

### Alternatives Considered
What other approaches were evaluated?

### Migration Path
How will this affect existing code?

### Complexity Impact
- Current API surface: X methods
- After addition: Y methods
- Justification for growth
```

### 2. Review Process

1. **Community Input**: 1-week comment period
2. **Technical Review**: Core team evaluation
3. **Complexity Assessment**: Impact on API surface
4. **Documentation Review**: Ensures clarity
5. **Decision**: Accept/Reject/Revise

### 3. Deprecation Policy

When removing or changing APIs:

1. **Deprecation Notice**: Mark with `@deprecated` tag
2. **Migration Guide**: Provide clear upgrade path
3. **Warning Period**: Minimum 3 minor versions
4. **Console Warnings**: Runtime deprecation notices
5. **Final Removal**: Only in major version

Example:
```typescript
/**
 * @deprecated Since 1.5.0. Use `trial()` instead.
 * Will be removed in 2.0.0.
 * 
 * Migration guide: https://docs.psyforge.org/migration/createTrial
 */
createTrial(config: TrialConfig): Trial {
  console.warn('createTrial is deprecated. Use trial() instead.');
  return trial(config);
}
```

## Monitoring

### Complexity Metrics

Track monthly:
- Total public methods
- Average method parameters
- API documentation coverage
- Deprecation count
- Community satisfaction

### User Feedback

Regular surveys on:
- API discoverability
- Learning curve
- Feature completeness
- Pain points

## Success Criteria

- API surface grows <10% per minor version
- 90% of experiments use Core API only
- JSON format handles 50% of use cases
- Zero breaking changes in minor versions
- Community satisfaction >4/5

## Timeline

- **Month 1**: Implement API linting rules
- **Month 2**: JSON format v1.0
- **Month 3**: First API addition under RFC process
- **Month 6**: Review and adjust process

## Alternatives Considered

1. **Feature Freeze**: Rejected - Limits innovation
2. **Separate APIs**: Rejected - Fragments ecosystem
3. **GraphQL-style**: Rejected - Over-engineered for use case

## References

- [Fluent Interface Pattern](https://martinfowler.com/bliki/FluentInterface.html)
- [API Design Principles](https://github.com/microsoft/api-guidelines)
- [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/)

# Migration Guide: [Feature Name] v[Old Version] → v[New Version]

**Status**: Active  
**Deprecation Date**: [Date when deprecated]  
**Removal Date**: [Date when will be removed]  
**Affected APIs**: [List of affected APIs]

## Overview

Brief description of what changed and why.

## Breaking Changes

List any breaking changes (only in major versions).

## Deprecated APIs

### `oldMethodName()`

**Deprecated in**: v[Version]  
**Will be removed in**: v[Version]  
**Replacement**: `newMethodName()`

#### Before (Deprecated)
```typescript
experiment()
  .oldMethodName(param1, param2)
  .build();
```

#### After (Recommended)
```typescript
experiment()
  .newMethodName({
    option1: param1,
    option2: param2
  })
  .build();
```

#### Migration Steps
1. Update imports if necessary
2. Replace method calls as shown above
3. Test thoroughly

## New Features

### Enhanced API
Description of new capabilities that replace old ones.

## Common Migration Patterns

### Pattern 1: Simple Replacement
```typescript
// Old
const exp = createExperiment({ /* config */ });

// New
const exp = experiment().configure({ /* config */ }).build();
```

### Pattern 2: Structural Changes
```typescript
// Old (multiple parameters)
trial.addStimulus('text', 'Hello', 1000);

// New (configuration object)
trial.stimulus({
  type: 'text',
  content: 'Hello',
  duration: 1000
});
```

## Automated Migration

For large codebases, use our migration tool:

```bash
npx @psyforge/migrate --from 1.0 --to 2.0 --dir ./src
```

## Validation

After migration, validate your experiments:

```bash
npx @psyforge/validate --check-deprecated
```

## FAQ

### Q: Will my existing experiments still work?
A: Yes, deprecated APIs will continue working until the removal date with console warnings.

### Q: How do I suppress deprecation warnings?
A: Set `PSYFORGE_SUPPRESS_DEPRECATION=true` in your environment (not recommended).

### Q: Where can I get help?
A: 
- GitHub Issues: [Link]
- Discord: [Link]
- Documentation: [Link]

## Rollback Plan

If you need to rollback:

1. Revert package version: `npm install @psyforge/forgescript@1.x`
2. Remove any new API usage
3. Test thoroughly

## Timeline

- **[Date]**: Deprecation announced
- **[Date]**: Migration guide published
- **[Date]**: Automated migration tool available
- **[Date]**: Last version with deprecated API
- **[Date]**: Deprecated API removed

---

*This migration guide will be updated as we receive feedback. Last updated: [Date]*

## Completed Items

content.text:
## Summary

I've successfully completed the first three tasks from Sprint 3's Critical Action Items:

### 1. ✅ Hardware Timing Validation Suite (TIMING-001)
Created a comprehensive `@psyforge/validation` package that includes:
- **HardwareBenchmark** class for measuring FPS, timing precision, memory usage, and frame variance
- **VideoFrameValidator** for testing requestVideoFrameCallback API support
- **CIValidator** for automated CI/CD integration with regression detection
- **CLI tool** (`npx psyforge-validate`) for easy validation runs
- Multiple output formats (JSON, Markdown, JUnit XML)
- Validates <2ms timing precision claims across different hardware configurations

### 2. ✅ WebGL2 Feature Detection and Fallback (COMPAT-001)
This was already implemented in the existing codebase:
- **EnhancedStimulusRenderer** has complete WebGL2/WebGL1/Canvas2D detection
- Automatic fallback mechanism when WebGL is unavailable
- Clear warning messages about reduced capabilities
- Comprehensive test coverage in WebGLFallback.test.ts

### 3. ✅ API Complexity Management (API-001)
Implemented a comprehensive API management system:
- **RFC-001** process for formal API addition proposals
- **JSON declarative format** as a simpler alternative to fluent API
- **JSON to ForgeScript compiler** for converting declarative experiments
- **API surface linter** using TypeScript AST analysis
- **Deprecation policy** with migration guide templates
- Progressive disclosure with Core/Extended/Expert API separation

The progress has been documented in progress.md, and Sprint 3 is now 60% complete. The next task to work on is Security Hardening Phase 2 (SEC-002).
